title: The skills that will be worthless in AI (and what to learn instead)
post:
Unpopular opinion: stop learning skills that AI will replace.

Worthless in 3 years:
- Memorizing syntax (AI writes better code)
- Basic data entry and processing (automated)
- Following documented procedures (AI follows them better)
- Writing boilerplate content (AI does this instantly

Valuable in 3 years:
- Prompt engineering and AI collaboration
- Systems thinking and architecture
- Critical evaluation of AI outputs
- Creative problem framing
- Human-centered skills: leadership, empathy, negotiation

The people who will struggle are those who doubled down on routine cognitive work. The people who thrive will be those who learned to work with AI, not against it.

What skills are you prioritizing this year?
